back_translation: When the kingdom of Rehoboam was established and he was strong, he forsook the law of the LORD, and all Israel with him.
In the fifth year of King Rehoboam, because they had been unfaithful to the LORD, Shishak king of Egypt came up against Jerusalem,
with 1,200 chariots and 60,000 horsemen, and the troops who came with him from Egypt were without number—Libyans, Sukkites, and Cushites.
He captured the fortified cities of Judah and came as far as Jerusalem.

Then Shemaiah the prophet came to Rehoboam and the leaders of Judah who had gathered at Jerusalem because of Shishak, and said to them, “Thus says the LORD: ‘You have forsaken Me, so I have forsaken you into the hand of Shishak.’”
Then the leaders of Israel and the king humbled themselves and said, “The LORD is righteous.”

When the LORD saw that they had humbled themselves, the word of the LORD came to Shemaiah: “They have humbled themselves; I will not destroy them, but I will grant them deliverance soon. My wrath shall not be poured out on Jerusalem by the hand of Shishak.
Nevertheless, they shall be his servants, that they may learn the difference between serving Me and serving the kingdoms of the lands.”
So Shishak king of Egypt came up against Jerusalem and seized the treasures of the house of the LORD and of the royal palace; he took everything, including the gold shields Solomon had made.
King Rehoboam made bronze shields in their place and committed them to the care of the captains of the guard who kept the entrance of the royal palace.
Whenever the king entered the house of the LORD, the guards would carry the shields, then return them to the guardroom.
When he humbled himself, the wrath of the LORD turned away from him, so as not to destroy him completely; and conditions in Judah were still good.

So King Rehoboam strengthened his royal power in Jerusalem and reigned. He was forty-one years old when he became king, and he reigned seventeen years in Jerusalem, the city the LORD had chosen from all the tribes of Israel to put His name there. His mother's name was Naamah the Ammonite.
And he did evil, because he did not set his heart to seek the LORD.

Now the acts of Rehoboam, from first to last, are they not written in the records of Shemaiah the prophet and of Iddo the seer, in the genealogies? And there were wars between Rehoboam and Jeroboam continually.
Rehoboam rested with his fathers and was buried in the City of David; and Abijah his son reigned in his place.
